questions.q: A point of $\mathbb{P}^2(K)$ is best described as:
choices: a line through the origin in $K^3$
a plane through the origin in $K^3$
questions.explain: $\mathbb{P}^2(K)=(K^3\setminus\{0\})/\sim$ where $(X,Y,Z)\sim(\lambda X,\lambda Y,\lambda Z)$ for $\lambda\in K^\times$. Each equivalence class is exactly a 1-dimensional linear subspace — a line through the origin in $K^3$.

questions.q: Which homogeneous triple represents the SAME point of $\mathbb{P}^2$ as $[2:4:6]$?
questions.explain: Two triples represent the same projective point iff one is a non-zero scalar multiple of the other. $[3:6:9]=\tfrac{3}{2}\cdot[2:4:6]$.

questions.q: Over the finite field $\mathbb{F}_q$, the number of points of $\mathbb{P}^2$ is $q^2+q+1$. How many points does $\mathbb{P}^2(\mathbb{F}_3)$ have?
What is the homogenization of the affine cubic $f(x,y)=y^2-x^3-1$?
questions.explain: For degree $d=3$, $F(X,Y,Z)=Z^3 f(X/Z,Y/Z)=Y^2Z-X^3-Z^3$. Each monomial ends at total degree $3$.

questions.q: The affine conic $f(x,y)=x^2+y^2-1$ homogenizes to $F(X,Y,Z)=X^2+Y^2-Z^2$. How many points at infinity ($Z=0$) does it have over $\mathbb{R}$?
questions.explain: Setting $Z=0$ gives $X^2+Y^2=0$, which over $\mathbb{R}$ forces $X=Y=0$ — not a valid projective point. So there are $0$ real points at infinity. (Over $\mathbb{C}$ there are two: $[1:\pm i:0]$.)

questions.q: The parabola $y-x^2=0$ homogenizes to $YZ-X^2=0$. How many points at infinity does it have?
questions.explain: Setting $Z=0$ in $YZ-X^2=0$ gives $X^2=0$, hence $X=0$ and $[0:1:0]$ is the unique projective solution. The parabola has exactly one point at infinity.

questions.q: The automorphism group of $\mathbb{P}^2(K)$ acting by homogeneous coordinate changes is:
questions.explain: Invertible $3\times3$ matrices act on $\mathbb{P}^2$, but two matrices give the same map iff they differ by a nonzero scalar. The quotient is $\mathrm{PGL}_3(K)=\mathrm{GL}_3(K)/K^\times$.

questions.q: What is the dimension of $\mathrm{PGL}_3(K)$ as an algebraic group over $K$?
questions.explain: $\dim\mathrm{GL}_3=9$ and we quotient by the $1$-dimensional center $K^\times$, giving $\dim\mathrm{PGL}_3=9-1=8$.

questions.q: A smooth projective plane conic over $\bar K$ is classified up to projective equivalence by:
choices: exactly one class — every smooth conic is projectively equivalent
a single integer invariant, its degree
questions.explain: Over an algebraically closed field any smooth plane conic can be brought into $X^2+Y^2=Z^2$ (or equivalently $XY=Z^2$) by an element of $\mathrm{PGL}_3$, so there is a unique projective equivalence class.

questions.q: The dual projective plane $(\mathbb{P}^2)^*$ is naturally identified with:
choices: the set of smooth conics in $\mathbb{P}^2$
questions.explain: A line $aX+bY+cZ=0$ is determined by $[a:b:c]\in\mathbb{P}^2$, so lines in $\mathbb{P}^2$ form another copy of $\mathbb{P}^2$, the dual plane $(\mathbb{P}^2)^*$.

questions.q: Under projective duality, the dual statement of "any two distinct points determine a unique line" is:
choices: any two distinct points determine a unique point
any two distinct lines meet in a unique point
every conic passes through three points
questions.explain: Swapping "point" $\leftrightarrow$ "line" and "lies on" $\leftrightarrow$ "passes through" turns "two points span one line" into "two lines meet in one point" — exactly the statement that $\mathbb{P}^2$ has no parallels.

questions.q: Over $\mathbb{F}_q$, the number of lines in $\mathbb{P}^2(\mathbb{F}_q)$ equals $|(\mathbb{P}^2)^*(\mathbb{F}_q)|=q^2+q+1$. How many lines does $\mathbb{P}^2(\mathbb{F}_2)$ contain?
questions.explain: By duality $|(\mathbb{P}^2)^*(\mathbb{F}_q)|=|\mathbb{P}^2(\mathbb{F}_q)|=q^2+q+1$. For $q=2$: $4+2+1=7$ — the Fano plane has $7$ points and $7$ lines, beautifully self-dual.

questions.q: Which statement about the cross-ratio of four distinct collinear points is TRUE?
choices: It depends on the choice of affine chart
It is invariant under every element of $\mathrm{PGL}_2(K)$ acting on the line
It equals the sum of the four coordinates
questions.explain: The cross-ratio is the unique (up to functional relation) projective invariant of four points on a line: any $g\in\mathrm{PGL}_2(K)$ acts by Möbius transformations, which preserve $[a,b;c,d]$.

questions.q: How many distinct values does the cross-ratio take as the four input points are permuted by $S_4$? (Equivalently: $|S_4|/|V_4|$ where $V_4$ is the Klein four-group of permutations that fix the cross-ratio.)
questions.explain: Of the $24$ permutations of four points, the Klein four-group $V_4$ (double-transpositions together with the identity) fixes the cross-ratio $\lambda$. The remaining $24/4=6$ cosets give the six values $\{\lambda,\ 1-\lambda,\ 1/\lambda,\ 1/(1-\lambda),\ \lambda/(\lambda-1),\ (\lambda-1)/\lambda\}$.
